title: ActiveMQ Temporary Storage Limit Pressure
dataSources: JMX `org.apache.activemq:type=Broker` `TempLimit`, `TempUsage` (percent or bytes depending on collector normalization)
app: Splunk Add-on for JMX (Splunkbase 2647) with ActiveMQ templates or scripted Jolokia→HEC (`sourcetype=activemq:broker`)
spl:
index=messaging sourcetype="activemq:broker"
| eval temp_pct=coalesce(TempPercentUsage, round(temp_used/temp_limit*100,1))
| where temp_pct > 70
| timechart span=5m max(temp_pct) as temp_usage_pct by broker_name
description: Non-persistent and slow-consumer spillover lands in temp storage; hitting `TempLimit` blocks producers even when the persistent store is healthy.
value: Isolates slow-consumer and large-message issues before they convert into broker-wide producer blocking.
implementation: Map JMX attributes to Splunk fields (`TempLimit`, `TempUsage`, or percent usage). Poll every 60s. Correlate with destination memory usage and consumer advisories. Alert at 70% sustained.
visualization: Gauge (temp %), Line chart (temp usage trend), Table (brokers over threshold).
grandmaExplanation: We watch the overflow storage area so a burst of messages does not lock the whole system when the main storage is still fine.
